**Royal Albert Football Club** are a [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club, historically based in the town of [Larkhall](/wiki/Larkhall "Larkhall"), [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland "Scotland") and are now playing in the nearby village of [Stonehouse](/wiki/Stonehouse,_South_Lanarkshire "Stonehouse, South Lanarkshire"). Formerly a member of the [Scottish Football League](/wiki/Scottish_Football_League "Scottish Football League") and the [Scottish Junior Football Association, West Region](/wiki/Scottish_Junior_Football_Association,_West_Region "Scottish Junior Football Association, West Region"), they now play in the [West of Scotland League Fourth Division](/wiki/West_of_Scotland_Football_League#Fourth_Division "West of Scotland Football League").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Albert_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The formation of the club dates back to 1878, when two Larkhall pit sides joined to form one team. These pits were owned by a Capt. Johns and he named the team after his boat the *Royal Albert*. The club spent over fifty years in Senior football, and qualified for the [Scottish Cup](/wiki/Scottish_Cup "Scottish Cup") on twenty occasions. Their greatest run came in the [1890–91](/wiki/1890%E2%80%9391_Scottish_Cup "1890–91 Scottish Cup") competition where they reached the fifth round before losing to [Celtic](/wiki/Celtic_F.C. "Celtic F.C.") in front of a record crowd of 5,000 at their [Raploch Park](/wiki/Raploch_Park "Raploch Park") ground.[[3]](#cite_note-lhsc-3)[[4]](#cite_note-UD-4) In the same season, [centre half](/wiki/Centre_half "Centre half") [John Clelland](/wiki/John_Clelland "John Clelland") became the clubs' one and only full [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland_national_football_team "Scotland national football team") international when he was capped against [Ireland](/wiki/Ireland_national_football_team_(1882%E2%80%931950) "Ireland national football team (1882–1950)") in March 1891.[[5]](#cite_note-5)[[6]](#cite_note-6) Royal Albert also have the distinction of scoring the first ever [penalty kick](/wiki/Penalty_kick_(association_football) "Penalty kick (association football)") in the history of football, James McLuggage scoring against [Airdrieonians](/wiki/Airdrieonians_F.C._(1878) "Airdrieonians F.C. (1878)") in a local cup tie on 6 June 1891 (four days after the rule was introduced by the [International Football Association Board](/wiki/International_Football_Association_Board "International Football Association Board")).[[7]](#cite_note-7)[[8]](#cite_note-8)

The club competed in various leagues such as the [Scottish Football Federation](/wiki/Scottish_Football_Federation "Scottish Football Federation"), [Scottish Football Alliance](/wiki/Scottish_Football_Alliance "Scottish Football Alliance"), Scottish Football Combination, [Scottish Football Union](/wiki/Scottish_Football_Union "Scottish Football Union") and Western Football League before becoming a founder member of [Scottish League Division Three](/wiki/Scottish_Football_League#History "Scottish Football League") in 1923. After the Third Division was abandoned in 1926, Albert briefly returned to the Scottish Football Alliance and Provincial Football League before being wound up in 1927 and re-forming as a [Junior](/wiki/Scottish_Junior_Football_Association "Scottish Junior Football Association") club the following year.[[9]](#cite_note-9)[[10]](#cite_note-10)[[11]](#cite_note-11)[[12]](#cite_note-12) The Royal Albert name continued to appear in the [Scottish Qualifying Cup](/wiki/Scottish_Qualifying_Cup "Scottish Qualifying Cup") until 1936 and they qualified for the tournament on two further occasions, their last game being a 16–0 thrashing by [Partick Thistle](/wiki/Partick_Thistle_F.C. "Partick Thistle F.C.") in January 1931.[[3]](#cite_note-lhsc-3)

Royal Albert initially competed in Junior football with some success and twice reached the semi-final of the [Scottish Junior Cup](/wiki/Scottish_Junior_Cup "Scottish Junior Cup") in 1930 and 1940 but fared less well in modern times, with the club playing in the bottom tier of the Central Region/District league set-up for 45 consecutive seasons from 1972 until 2018.[[13]](#cite_note-13)[[14]](#cite_note-14) Albert won the last [West of Scotland League Central District Second Division](/wiki/West_of_Scotland_League_Central_District_Second_Division "West of Scotland League Central District Second Division") championship title in 2017–18 to qualify for the new [SJFA West Region League One](/wiki/SJFA_West_Region_League_One "SJFA West Region League One") and in doing so, lifted the club's first honour for 81 years.[[15]](#cite_note-15)

Some notable players in the Junior era to step up from the club include [Bobby Hogg](/wiki/Bobby_Hogg_(footballer,_born_1914) "Bobby Hogg (footballer, born 1914)") ([Celtic](/wiki/Celtic_F.C. "Celtic F.C.")) and [John Martis](/wiki/John_Martis "John Martis") ([Motherwell](/wiki/Motherwell_F.C. "Motherwell F.C.")) with both playing for [Scotland](/wiki/Scotland_national_football_team "Scotland national football team") later in their careers, [George Miller](/wiki/George_Miller_(footballer_born_1939) "George Miller (footballer born 1939)"), who won the [Scottish Cup](/wiki/Scottish_Cup "Scottish Cup") in [1961](/wiki/1960%E2%80%9361_Scottish_Cup "1960–61 Scottish Cup") with [Dunfermline Athletic](/wiki/Dunfermline_Athletic_F.C. "Dunfermline Athletic F.C.") and [Davie White](/wiki/David_White_(Scottish_footballer) "David White (Scottish footballer)") who played for and managed [Clyde](/wiki/Clyde_F.C. "Clyde F.C.") before managing [Rangers](/wiki/Rangers_F.C. "Rangers F.C.").[[16]](#cite_note-16)[[17]](#cite_note-17)[[18]](#cite_note-18) Hogg is also Royal Albert's most capped [Junior international](/wiki/Scottish_Junior_Football_Association#Scotland_Junior_international_team "Scottish Junior Football Association") with three caps in 1931,[[19]](#cite_note-19)

The team have been managed since June 2016 by former player Jamie Nesbitt.[[20]](#cite_note-20) Nesbitt succeeded Ian McCluskey, who had filled the role for the previous 12 years.[[21]](#cite_note-21)

## Ground

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Royal_Albert_F.C.&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Ground")]

Albert's first ground was [Raploch Park](/wiki/Raploch_Park "Raploch Park") which was lost to a housing development in the 1960s. The club moved to the Robert Smillie Memorial Park in 1964 but were forced to vacate this ground at the end of the 2006–07 season as it was demolished in order to re-develop Larkhall Academy.[[22]](#cite_note-22) The club ground-shared with local rivals [Larkhall Thistle](/wiki/Larkhall_Thistle_F.C. "Larkhall Thistle F.C.") until 2013. After a proposed move to nearby [Ashgill](/wiki/Ashgill "Ashgill") fell through, the club have moved to Tileworks Park, former home of [Stonehouse Violet](/wiki/Stonehouse_Violet_F.C. "Stonehouse Violet F.C."), on a permanent basis.[[23]](#cite_note-23)
